title: Le Monde's point-by-point breakdown of the fragile US-Iran framework agreement
subhead: After more than 100 days of war, the United States and Iran signed a memorandum of understanding aimed at ending their conflict. The document will be followed by negotiations to address issues it does not resolve. Le Monde publishes the full text, along with context and analysis.

Israel and Hezbollah agree to ceasefire after 47 Lebanese killed in Israeli airstrikes
subhead: The violence, which also left four Israeli soldiers dead, is the worst since the sealing of a US-Iran deal to halt the wider Middle East war, which was supposed to also pause fighting between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon.

title: Heatwave hits more than half of France's population
subhead: Nearly 36 million French people, more than one in two, were affected by an orange heat alert issued by the weather office, the second-highest such warning urging residents to be 'very cautious.'

title: Mozart manuscript discovered at French National Library to be performed for the first time
subhead: The music notebook, found in the archives, dates from the composer's final stay in Paris, in 1778. The seven short pieces for flute and harp it contains will be broadcast for the first time ever on June 22 on France Musique radio.

title: 'They call us misfits, degenerates': Free parties fall under French government scrutiny
subhead: Thousands of people gathered on June 6 in the south of France for a rave party, despite the events being banned locally until the end of the year. Participants defend them as among the last spaces of freedom outside of commercial and institutional frameworks.

In Trump's polarized America, empathy faces unanimous opposition
subhead: Once valued in American public life, empathy now faces criticism from the far right, which sees it as a sign of weakness. The left, too, is questioning its relevance: In a world where brutality prevails, can we still afford to show understanding?

title: Ending the war with Iran comes at the cost of major US concessions
subhead: The memorandum of understanding, signed on the evening of June 17 by the presidents of both countries is intended to end hostilities and allow for the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. Nuclear negotiations will begin immediately afterward.

title: French security replaces Palantir with domestic firm ChapsVision, a complicated choice
subhead: Used since the 2015 terrorist attacks, the controversial American company's data analysis software is set to be gradually replaced by that of a French provider. The long-awaited decision presents a significant technological challenge.
